appeal.appealJustification: Attached is a rate sheet obtained from the gym confirming the $30 group-class rate. The class is open to the public and subject-based (martial arts), consistent with OPWDD community-class guidelines. We respectfully request reconsideration of the denial.
appeal.denialReason: Published fees could not be verified on the provider's website.

parsed.justification: Attached is a rate sheet obtained from the gym confirming the $30 group-class rate. The class is open to the public and subject-based (martial arts), consistent with OPWDD community-class guidelines. We respectfully request reconsideration of the denial.
parsed.valuedOutcome: Yosef will improve physical fitness, self-regulation, and social participation through a structured martial-arts class.

category.label: Pre-Approval Appeal (Community Class)
capNotes: Invoice guideline: >2 sessions/day (up to 60 min each) or >7 sessions/week at one provider requires prior approval.

webChecks.label: Class is open to and attended by the broader public
webChecks.criteria: Look for evidence that the class is offered to the general public (public class schedules, 'all welcome', open enrollment, drop-in info). It must NOT be restricted to disabled/OPWDD individuals.

webChecks.label: Class has published fees
webChecks.criteria: A concrete public price for the class must be visible on the site (e.g. '$30 per class', '10-class pack $250'). A 'contact us for pricing' page means the fee is NOT published.

webChecks.label: Fees are identical for OPWDD and non-OPWDD individuals
webChecks.criteria: Check whether the published pricing applies to everyone. If the site shows one public price list with no special disabled/OPWDD rate, this is supported. If a special/segregated price for disabled participants exists, this fails. If pricing is not published at all, mark Needs Review.

webChecks.label: Class is subject-based (art, dance, martial arts, cooking, etc.)
webChecks.criteria: The class must teach a concrete subject or skill (art, dance, martial arts, cooking, riding, music...). Generic supervision or day-hab style programming does not count.

webChecks.label: Class does not give college credits
webChecks.criteria: Confirm the class is non-credit. If the provider is a college/university, look for 'non-credit', 'continuing education', or absence of credit language. If credit-bearing, this fails.

webChecks.label: Class is not clinical/therapy in nature
webChecks.criteria: The class must be recreational/educational, not therapy. Words like 'therapy', 'treatment', 'clinical', 'licensed therapist' describing the service itself indicate failure. Note: 'therapeutic riding' style marketing needs care - if the offering reviewed is the recreational program, judge that program.

webChecks.label: A published schedule of the class exists
webChecks.criteria: Look for a class schedule/timetable (days, times, or session calendar) publicly visible on the site.

webChecks.label: Published fee matches the fee stated on the form
webChecks.criteria: Compare the fee stated on the application to the fee published on the website. State both amounts. Verdict must be one of: matches application exactly / differs / not published.

category.appealInstructions: This is an APPEAL of a previous denial. Re-run every Community Classes website check, but frame the whole review against the stated denial reason. For each check, and especially any check related to the denial reason, explicitly state whether the website evidence SUPPORTS or REFUTES the denial reason. Evidence the appellant says exists offline (e.g. a rate sheet obtained from the gym) cannot be verified from the website - acknowledge it in notes but do not count it as website evidence.

findings.note: The website explicitly states that Jiu-Jitsu is for everyone and offers classes for all ages and skill levels, indicating it is open to the general public. This evidence supports the general eligibility of the class.
findings.label: Class is open to and attended by the broader public

findings.note: No specific fees or prices for any classes (group or private) are published on the provided website pages. The website repeatedly offers a 'Free Class' but no ongoing pricing. This evidence supports the original denial reason that published fees could not be verified on the provider's website.
findings.label: Class has published fees

findings.note: Since no fees are published on the website, it is not possible to determine if they are identical for OPWDD and non-OPWDD individuals. This indirectly supports the original denial reason regarding the inability to verify published fees.
findings.label: Fees are identical for OPWDD and non-OPWDD individuals

findings.note: The class is clearly described as Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, a martial art, focusing on techniques, self-defence skills, and personal growth. This evidence supports the general eligibility of the class.
findings.quote: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu (BJJ), often called the “Gentle Art,” is a martial art rooted in leverage, technique, and strategy over brute strength.
findings.label: Class is subject-based (art, dance, martial arts, cooking, etc.)

findings.note: The website describes Gracie Barra as a martial arts academy and Jiu-Jitsu organization, with no mention of college, university, or academic credits. This evidence supports the general eligibility of the class.
findings.quote: Gracie Barra is not just a martial arts academy; it’s a legacy deeply rooted in Jiu-Jitsu history.
findings.label: Class does not give college credits

findings.note: The descriptions consistently refer to martial arts training, self-defense, fitness, and personal growth, not clinical therapy or treatment. This evidence supports the general eligibility of the class.
findings.quote: Through structured training, students develop not only self-defence skills but also confidence, resilience, and lifelong fitness.
findings.label: Class is not clinical/therapy in nature

findings.note: While various class programs are described, no public schedule with specific days and times for classes is available on the provided pages. This evidence does not directly relate to the denial reason about published fees, but indicates a lack of comprehensive information.
findings.label: A published schedule of the class exists

findings.note: The website does not publish any specific fees for group classes or private sessions. It only offers a 'Book Your Free Class'. Therefore, the stated fee of $30 per session cannot be matched. This evidence strongly supports the original denial reason.
findings.label: Published fee matches the fee stated on the form

rateComparison.note: The website does not publish any specific fees for group classes or private sessions. It only offers a 'Book Your Free Class'.

appealAssessment: The website evidence supports the stated denial reason: 'Published fees could not be verified on the provider's website.' Across all provided pages, no specific fees or prices for any classes, including adult group Jiu Jitsu, are published. The website consistently promotes a 'Book Your Free Class' but provides no pricing information for ongoing participation. The appellant's justification mentions an 'attached rate sheet obtained from the gym,' but this is offline evidence and cannot be verified through the website.

GRACIE BARRA
JIU-JITSU FOR EVERYONE®
Getting started at Gracie Barra is easy
Book Your Free Class
We Are Gracie Barra

Gracie Barra is not just a martial arts academy; it’s a legacy deeply rooted in Jiu-Jitsu history. Founded by Master Carlos Gracie Jr. in 1986, Gracie Barra has grown into one of the largest and most respected Jiu-Jitsu organizations worldwide.

Learn more about us

What is Jiu-Jitsu?

Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu (BJJ), often called the “Gentle Art,” is a martial art rooted in leverage, technique, and strategy over brute strength. It empowers individuals of all ages and body types to defend themselves effectively - without relying on size or power.

At its core, Jiu-Jitsu is about control, discipline, and personal growth.

Through structured training, students develop not only self-defence skills but also confidence, resilience, and lifelong fitness. It's a journey of mental and physical transformation—on and off the mats.

Learn practical self-defence
Build discipline, focus, and resilience
Improve fitness, coordination, and flexibility
Join a supportive community that helps you grow
Start training today
"My life is dedicated to Jiu-Jitsu. My goal was simply to build a brotherhood to lead the expansion of BJJ while respecting the essence of our art. My students are an extension of my family. I want each one of them to achieve their personal best while helping spread Jiu-Jitsu. We must do that by honoring the traditions and efforts of those who came before us."
- Master Carlos Gracie Jr
Begin Your Jiu-Jitsu Journey With Gracie Barra
Book Your Free Class

Our Vision & Mission

With over 1,000 schools globally, our vision is to have one Gracie Barra school in every community, sharing the values of brotherhood, integrity, and development through the highest level of Jiu-Jitsu instruction.

At Gracie Barra, our mission is clear: to teach Jiu-Jitsu for Everyone. Founded by Master Carlos Gracie Jr. in 1986, we believe Jiu-Jitsu is a tool to train the body, mind, and spirit—empowering individuals and strengthening communities around the world.

Why Gracie Barra?

Become a Gracie Barra School Owner

Learn More About Opening A Gracie Barra School

Open A GB School

What Sets Us Apart

Gracie Barra is unique because we are the largest Jiu-Jitsu team in the world, with over 1,000 schools across six continents; we follow a structured curriculum designed for safe, effective learning; and our Red Shield represents a global family united by shared values.

To further strengthen our community, uphold our philosophy, and support school growth, Gracie Barra is powered by a network of purpose-driven organizations:

High-performance uniforms and apparel that reinforce unity and professionalism across all GB schools.
The GB Institute provides educational resources to instructors, students, and school owners. Through structured leadership and training programs, we help develop the next generation of Jiu-Jitsu instructors and business leaders.
Train anywhere, anytime. GB Online offers video instruction, techniques, and training resources to enhance your learning experience beyond the mats.
Bringing together school owners, instructors, and students to learn, grow, and connect—through Summits, Camps, and leadership conferences.
Gracie Barra’s exclusive tournament league offers a safe, structured competition experience for members of all skill levels.

GB HomepageClasses
CLASSES

Gracie Barra offers progressive Jiu-Jitsu training for students of all ages and skill levels. Whether you're just beginning your journey or already an experienced practitioner, our structured classes are designed to support your development every step of the way.

Gracie Barra Kids

Our GBK Program introduces children to Jiu-Jitsu in a fun, safe, and age-appropriate classes - building confidence, discipline, and respect through structured curriculum. It focuses on self-defence, takedowns, and ground techniques.

Jiu-Jitsu for Women

The GBF Program offers a supportive and inclusive introduction to Jiu-Jitsu for women, focusing on core techniques while building confidence and preparing students to progress into more advanced classes like GB1, GB2 and GB3.

Jiu-Jitsu Fundamentals

The GB1 Program is the foundation of Jiu-Jitsu at Gracie Barra. Originally called the "Fundamental Program," it offers structured training in self-defence, core techniques, and the philosophy of Gracie Barra. Designed for beginners, GB1 builds strong technical skills and prepares students for progression to advanced levels.

Advanced Jiu-Jitsu

The GB2 Program builds on fundamentals with advanced techniques, complex drills, No-Gi training and live sessions - preparing students for GB3. Eligibility requires at least 3 stripes on a white belt, which requires a minimum of 4 months of training.

Expert Jiu-Jitsu

The GB3 Program is the ultimate level of training at a GB school and challenges students, increasing the complexity of the drills, and the intensity of the training session. It is open to blue belts and above.

Private Classes

Private classes offer one-on-one training tailored to your goals - whether it's refining techniques, preparing for competition, or accelerating progress. With personalized training schedules and focused feedback, students of all levels gain deeper insight into their Jiu-Jitsu journey.

Begin Your Jiu-Jitsu Journey With Gracie Barra

GB HomepageClassesJiu-Jitsu Fundamentals
JIU-JITSU FUNDAMENTALS

The perfect starting point for anyone new to Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. GB1 teaches the essential techniques, mindset, and values to begin your journey with confidence.

New To Jiu-Jitsu? Start Here.

The GB1 Program is the foundation of Jiu-Jitsu at Gracie Barra - designed specifically for beginners. With no experience required, this program introduces students to core techniques, self-defence skills, and the philosophy behind our team.

Classes are structured, supportive, and paced to help you feel comfortable as you learn. Whether you’re looking to improve fitness, gain confidence, or simply try something new, GB1 gives you the tools to grow on the mats and beyond.

Our certified instructors focus on proper movement, posture, and mindset, helping you build strong fundamentals that prepare you for progression into GB2 and beyond.

Book Your Free Class
Class Benefits
Builds a strong foundation in Jiu-Jitsu techniques and self-defence
Improves fitness, coordination, and body awareness
Introduces the principles of Jiu-Jitsu for personal growth
Boosts confidence, focus, and emotional resilience
Promotes respect, discipline, and a strong sense of community
Encourages lasting friendships through teamwork and shared challenges

GB1 is more than just a training program - it’s your gateway into the world of Jiu-Jitsu. Each class supports both physical and mental wellbeing, offering structure, encouragement, and meaningful progress in every session.

What to Bring to Your First Class

Water Bottle

Bring a water bottle to stay hydrated during training.

Comfortable Clothing

Wear activewear like shorts, leggings, and a t-shirt that allows you to move freely. A Gracie Barra Gi and rashguard will be provided for your introductory class.

Slip-on Shoes

Flip-flops or sandals make it easy to keep the mats clean and transition in and out of class quickly.

Positive Attitude

Come ready to learn, have fun, and challenge yourself. Everyone starts somewhere, and our team is here to support you.

FAQs
Do I need experience to join GB1?
No experience is necessary! GB1 is designed for beginners and those new to Jiu-Jitsu.
Will I have to spar in my first class?
How long does it take to progress from GB1 to GB2?
